rows.notes: Page year at top, very clear and centered; no monetary columns present.
Clear heading under year. Handwriting legible; no amounts on this row.

rows.description: Of the Solicitor - Mr. Polls plate money
rows.notes: Amounts line up clearly in the pounds column (10) and zeroes in s and d columns are legible. The surname 'Polls' is readable but the final letter could be interpreted differently (e.g. 'Polls' vs 'Ollis'); chosen 'Polls' based on stroke shape.
Aligned amounts read as £5 18s 8d. 'Do.' abbreviation and phrase 'Probate Deposit 1 yr' are clear; digits are crisp and within valid ranges.

rows.description: Of Mr Catterbuck, Grocer, Interest Demand for the 2 years ending at Xmas 1830
rows.notes: Large pounds figure (504) is well aligned and clear. The surname is somewhat ornate — read as 'Catterbuck' (could be 'Cotterbuck' or similar); identified as a grocer from the following word which is legible. Date phrase '2 years ending at Xmas 1830' is readable.

rows.description: Of Do. for the Bedmakers Fund to Do.
rows.notes: Amount £42 1s 0d aligns with pounds/shillings/pence columns cleanly. Text is an abbreviated ditto line; 'Bedmakers Fund' reading is clear.

rows.description: Of Mr Davis Materials sold at Shearmen being the Chapel Barn at Little ?
rows.notes: Amount £4 10s 0d is clear in the columns. The description is partially faded/ornate: 'Mr Davis' and 'Materials sold' are legible, but the place/name after 'Little' is uncertain (looks like 'Little Dos' or similar). Recorded text preserves uncertain trailing word and a question mark; lower confidence due to place-name ambiguity.

rows.description: Of Mr Lightfoot Deans Fees to Mids 1831
rows.notes: Monetary columns read as £47 13s 4d and are well-aligned. The name reads as 'Lightfoot' (script is fairly clear). 'Deans Fees to Mids 1831' interpreted from cursive 'Deans Fees to Mids. 1831' — slight uncertainty on abbreviation Mids but context supports it.

rows.description: Of Mr Harrington, Bursar, Treasurer Demand for the 2 years ending at Midsr. 1831
rows.notes: Large pounds figure (1044) and smaller shillings/pence read clearly as £1044 2s 6d. The personal name reads as 'Harrington' though the hand is ornate and could be 'Harington'; identified as 'Bursar, Treasurer' from adjacent words, with some uncertainty about exact honorifics.

rows.description: Of Do. for the Bedmakers Fund to Do.
rows.notes: Second instance of a Bedmakers Fund ditto line. Amount columns clearly read £41 10s 6d. Text is abbreviated but legible as a repeat of earlier Bedmakers Fund entry.

rows.description: Of Dr Shortridge's Trust - 1/2 years Rent to Mids 1830
rows.notes: Amount reads clearly as £110 0s 0d. Name interpreted as 'Dr Shortridge' — spelling could be 'Shortridge' or 'Shortridges'; the possessive and 'Trust' phrase are legible. '1/2 years Rent to Mids 1830' read from abbreviated script.

Second similar line also showing £110 0s 0d and aligned. The description text is faint and the middle word reads ambiguously as 'Annuat' or 'Arrear' — chosen 'Annuat' (annual) based on context but with moderate uncertainty.

rows.description: S. Newington Estate for General Repairs on the 1/2 year to Mich. 1829
rows.notes: Amount £25 0s 0d is clear. 'S. Newington Estate' and 'General Repairs' are legible; the dating '1/2 year to Mich. 1829' is reasonably clear (Mich = Michaelmas).
A repeated (ditto) line with amount £25 0s 0d clearly aligned. 'Ladyday 1830' is a clear calendar reference.

rows.description: one fourth of Improved Rent of Medeshaw on one years Rent to Annas 1829
rows.notes: Amount read as £15 13s 10½d (pence shows a clear half-penny loop). The property/place name 'Medeshaw' (or similar) is uncertain in spelling; wording 'one fourth of Improved Rent' and the date '1829' are legible. Moderate confidence due to place-name uncertainty.

rows.description: one fourth of Do. for Add Land Rents on the year to Mich. 1830
rows.notes: Amount interpreted as £15 9s 2½d. The pence digit and trailing fractional mark are faint — appears to be a half (similar loop to prior ½). The description uses ditto 'Do.' and 'Add Land Rents' which are partially ambiguous; therefore lower confidence.
Final total at bottom right clearly written as £1997 19s 6½d; alignment on pounds/shillings/pence columns is unambiguous. The half-penny symbol is clearly present. High confidence for numeric total.
